title: AI Guardian Configuration
description: Configuration file for ai-guardian security tool that protects IDE AI agents from unauthorized tool access and security risks

permissions.description: Tool permissions configuration - WHERE THE RULES LIVE. Controls whether AI Guardian enforces tool permission rules and defines the permission rules for tools (Skills, MCP servers, built-in tools like Bash/Write/Read). Works with permissions_directories (auto-discovery feeds INTO this section). Default: Built-in tools ALLOW, Skills and MCP servers BLOCK unless explicitly allowed. NEW unified structure in v1.4.0.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

auto_directory_rules.description: Auto-generate directory rules from skill permissions (NEW in v1.8.0, Issue #144). Eliminates duplication by automatically creating directory access rules for allowed skills. When enabled, scans standard skill locations and generates 'allow' directory rules for skills matching permission patterns. Generated rules are inserted FIRST (weakest) so user rules can override. Rule evaluation order: Generated → User → Immutable (last-match-wins). Opt-in feature - requires explicit enable.

enabled.description: Enable auto-generation of directory rules from skill permissions. Default: false (opt-in). When true, generates directory rules for allowed skills and inserts them at the beginning of directory_rules.rules array (before user rules). On first enable, shows interactive confirmation with preview.

oneOf.description: Auto-discover skill directories from standard locations
Explicit list of skill directory paths to scan

skill_directories.description: Skill directories to scan for auto-generation. Default: 'auto' scans standard locations (./.claude/skills, ~/.claude/skills, $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills). Can also provide explicit array of paths.

allow_symlinks.description: Allow following symlinks when discovering skills. Default: true. In container environments (e.g., carbonite), skills are installed as symlinks. When true, symlinked skill directories that resolve to valid directories are followed. When false, all symlinks are skipped (original behavior). Broken symlinks are always skipped regardless of this setting.

rules.description: Permission rules for tools (Skills, MCP servers, built-in tools like Bash/Write/Read). Default: Built-in tools ALLOW, Skills and MCP servers BLOCK unless explicitly allowed.

oneOf.description: Object format with allow/deny arrays (backward compatible)

deny.description: Directories to scan for deny patterns

allow.description: Directories to scan for allow patterns

oneOf.description: Array format with mode in each entry (NEW: mode field actually used)

permissions_directories.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Auto-discover tool permissions - HOW TO AUTO-POPULATE RULES. Scans directories/GitHub repos for permission files and merges discovered rules INTO permissions.rules. Data flow: scan directories → discover permission files → generate rules → merge into permissions.rules. NOT related to directory_rules (filesystem path access control). Most users should use remote_configs instead. Supports both object format (allow/deny arrays) and direct array format.

remote_configs.description: RECOMMENDED: Load policies from remote URLs (enterprise/team policies). This is the preferred way to manage permissions.

urls.description: List of remote configuration URLs to fetch

refresh_interval_hours.description: Hours between automatic refresh of remote configs

expire_after_hours.description: Hours until cached remote config expires

secret_scanning.description: Secret scanning with multi-engine support (v1.4.0+). Object with 'enabled' property that supports boolean (permanent) or time-based (temporary) formats. Multi-engine support added in v1.5.0 - configure multiple engines with automatic fallback.

engines.description: List of secret scanning engines to use. Tries engines in order, using first available. Supported: 'gitleaks' (default, industry standard), 'betterleaks' (faster fork), 'leaktk' (auto-pattern management), 'trufflehog' (700+ detectors with verification), 'detect-secrets' (baseline workflow), or custom engine config objects. See docs/MULTI_ENGINE_SUPPORT.md for examples.

oneOf.description: Custom engine configuration or preset override

command_template.description: Command template with placeholders (required for custom type)

success_exit_code.description: Exit code indicating no secrets found

execution_strategy.description: Strategy for executing multiple scanner engines (v1.6.0+). 'first-match' uses first available engine (default, backward compatible). 'any-match' runs all engines and blocks if ANY finds secrets (maximum security, defense-in-depth). 'consensus' blocks only if N engines agree (reduces false positives, configurable via consensus_threshold). See docs/MULTI_ENGINE_SUPPORT.md for use cases and examples.

consensus_threshold.description: Minimum number of engines that must agree before blocking when using 'consensus' execution strategy (v1.6.0+). For example, threshold=2 means at least 2 engines must find the same secret at the same location before blocking. Higher values reduce false positives but may miss some secrets. Only used when execution_strategy='consensus'.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for test fixtures, examples, and documentation with fake credentials. Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Examples: '**/tests/fixtures/**', '**/examples/**/*.example.*', '**/.gitleaks.toml'

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for tools that read test data or documentation. Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: [] (scan all tools), specific use cases vary. Most users should use ignore_files instead for test fixtures.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Enhanced secret detection patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.7.0: nested under secret_scanning). Configure this section to use enterprise patterns instead of Gitleaks defaults. Presence = enabled, null or absence = disabled (use defaults). Supports cascade/fallback: pattern server → project .gitleaks.toml → Gitleaks defaults.

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED: Use presence/absence of pattern_server section instead. Will be removed in v2.0.0.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth errors, network errors, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this pattern_server section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

secret_redaction.description: Secret redaction configuration (NEW in v1.5.0). Redacts secrets from tool outputs instead of blocking them entirely, providing defense-in-depth while allowing work to continue. Part of Phase 4: Hermes Security Patterns integration.

enabled.description: Enable secret redaction for tool outputs

action.description: Action to take when secrets are detected: 'log-only' (redact silently), 'warn' (redact with user warning and notification)

preserve_format.description: Preserve format when redacting (e.g., 'sk-proj...901vwx' instead of '[REDACTED]')

log_redactions.description: Log redaction events to violation logger

additional_patterns.description: Custom secret patterns to redact in addition to built-in patterns

type.description: Human-readable name for this secret type

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Secret redaction patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise secret patterns that can be updated as new secret formats emerge. Supports override modes: 'replace' (use only server patterns) or 'extend' (add to defaults).

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for secret patterns (appended to url)

pattern_server.description: DEPRECATED: Move to secret_scanning.pattern_server (v1.7.0+). This root-level location is supported for backward compatibility but will be removed in v2.0.0.

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED: Use presence/absence of pattern_server section instead

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth errors, network errors, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

prompt_injection.description: Prompt injection detection configuration (NEW in v1.2.0, time-based support in v1.4.0). Protects against prompt injection attacks that try to manipulate AI behavior.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

action.description: Action on violation (NEW in v1.7.0): 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

detector.description: Detection method: heuristic (local patterns, <1ms), rebuff/llm-guard (ML-based, requires setup)

sensitivity.description: Detection sensitivity: low (obvious attacks only), medium (balanced), high (more aggressive)

allowlist_patterns.description: Regex patterns to ignore (for false positives). Supports time-based patterns.

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Example: '**/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md' to ignore all skill documentation files.

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: 'Skill:code-review' (ignore specific skill), 'Skill:*' or 'Skill' (ignore all skills), 'mcp__*' (ignore all MCP tools). Useful for tools that legitimately read documentation containing example attack patterns.

unicode_detection.description: Unicode-based attack detection configuration (NEW in Phase 2: Issue #195). Detects attacks using invisible characters, bidirectional overrides, tag characters, and homoglyphs that bypass pattern matching.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

detect_zero_width.description: Detect zero-width characters (9 types: zero-width space, joiner, non-joiner, etc.) that break pattern matching

detect_bidi_override.description: Detect bidirectional text override characters (RTL/LTR override) used for visual deception

detect_tag_chars.description: Detect Unicode tag characters (U+E0000 - U+E007F) used for hidden data encoding

detect_homoglyphs.description: Detect homoglyph substitutions (80+ look-alike character pairs: Cyrillic/Greek/Math symbols that look like Latin)

allow_rtl_languages.description: Allow legitimate RTL (right-to-left) languages like Arabic and Hebrew. When true, bidi overrides in RTL text blocks are allowed.

allow_emoji.description: Allow emoji with zero-width joiners (e.g., family emoji 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦). When true, zero-width joiners in emoji contexts are allowed.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Homoglyph patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise homoglyph patterns that can be updated as new scripts emerge. Immutable patterns (zero-width chars, bidi overrides) cannot be overridden.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for Unicode patterns (appended to url)

ssrf_protection.description: SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery) protection configuration (NEW in v1.5.0). Prevents AI agents from accessing private networks, cloud metadata endpoints, and dangerous URL schemes. Inspired by Hermes Security Framework.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

action.description: Action on violation: 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

additional_blocked_ips.description: Additional IP addresses or CIDR ranges to block (beyond core protections). Core protections (RFC 1918, loopback, link-local, metadata endpoints) cannot be disabled.

items.description: IP address or CIDR range (IPv4 or IPv6)

additional_blocked_domains.description: Additional domain names to block (beyond core protections). Core protections (metadata.google.internal, etc.) cannot be disabled. Supports exact domains, subdomain matching, and wildcard patterns (* and ? wildcards).

items.description: Domain name or wildcard pattern to block. Examples: 'exact.example.com' (exact + subdomains), '*.internal.com' (all .internal.com), 'admin.*' (admin with any suffix), '*.corp.*' (any .corp. domain), 'test?.example.com' (test1, test2, etc.)

allow_localhost.description: Allow localhost access (127.0.0.1, ::1). Default: false. Enable for local development scenarios.

allowed_domains.description: Domain allow-list to override deny-list blocks (evaluated AFTER deny-list). Allows specific trusted domains while maintaining core protections. Core protections (metadata endpoints, dangerous schemes) cannot be overridden. Supports exact and subdomain matching.

path_based_rules.description: Path-based filtering rules for granular access control (NEW in v1.6.0). Allows blocking/allowing specific URL paths on domains. Useful for allowing public API endpoints while blocking admin pages on the same domain. Evaluation order: 1) Domain-level checks (blocked_domains/allowed_domains), 2) Path rules (if domain has path rules defined). Supports glob patterns: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char).

domain.description: Domain to apply path rules to (e.g., 'api.example.com', 'internal.corp.local')

allowed_paths.description: URL paths to allow on this domain. If domain is blocked, these paths override the block. Glob patterns supported: /api/* (API endpoints), /public/** (recursive), /health (exact), /v?/users (version wildcard). Query parameters are included in matching.

blocked_paths.description: URL paths to block on this domain. If domain is allowed, these paths override the allow. Glob patterns supported: /admin/* (admin pages), /internal/** (recursive), /*.php (file extension), /*secret* (contains). Query parameters are included in matching.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: SSRF protection patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise patterns instead of hardcoded defaults. Supports three-tier system: immutable core (metadata endpoints, dangerous schemes) + pattern server/defaults (RFC 1918 ranges) + local config additions.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for SSRF patterns (appended to url)

allow_override.description: Allow pattern server to override default patterns (RFC 1918 ranges). Immutable patterns (metadata endpoints) cannot be overridden.

validate_critical.description: Validate that critical patterns are present in final config (auto-add if missing)

config_file_scanning.description: Config file scanning configuration (NEW in v1.5.0, Phase 3 of Hermes integration). Detects credential exfiltration commands in AI configuration files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules) that could cause persistent credential theft across all AI sessions. Scans for 8 core patterns: curl/wget with env vars, env|curl, printenv exfil, file exfil, base64 exfil, AWS S3, GCP Storage.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

action.description: Action on violation: 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

additional_files.description: Additional config file patterns to scan (beyond defaults: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, .aider.conf.yml, .github/CLAUDE.md). Examples: '.github/copilot-instructions.md', 'AI_INSTRUCTIONS.md', '.windsurf/rules.md'

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during config file scanning. Useful for documentation with security examples. Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char). Examples: '**/docs/security-examples.md', '**/examples/**'

additional_patterns.description: Additional regex patterns to detect (beyond 8 core patterns). Core patterns cannot be disabled: curl with env vars, wget with env vars, env|curl, printenv|curl, cat /etc/|curl, base64|curl, aws s3 cp, gcloud storage cp

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Config exfiltration patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise patterns for detecting credential exfiltration in AI config files. Immutable core patterns cannot be overridden.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for config exfiltration patterns (appended to url)

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED: Array format. Use object format with action field instead.

Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read. Order-based directory access control rules (NEW in v1.6.0). Rules are evaluated sequentially with last match winning. Supports flexible allow/deny patterns for enterprise skill allowlists and path restrictions. COMPLETELY SEPARATE from permissions_directories (which auto-discovers TOOL permissions, not path restrictions). Common confusion: permissions_directories = where to find tool permission config, directory_rules = which paths to protect.

action.description: Action on violation: 'block' prevents access (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows access, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning. Applies to ALL rules.

rules.description: List of directory access rules evaluated in order (last match wins)

mode.description: Access mode: 'allow' permits access, 'deny' blocks access

paths.description: Directory paths for this rule. Supports ~ expansion, ** for recursive, * for single level.

directory_exclusions.description: DEPRECATED: Use directory_rules instead. Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read. NOT related to permissions_directories (which auto-discovers TOOL permissions). Directory exclusions can override .ai-read-deny blocking (v1.5.0). Automatically converted to directory_rules internally.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this entire section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

paths.description: Directory paths to exclude from .ai-read-deny blocking (exclusions override deny markers). Supports ~ expansion, ** for recursive, * for single level. Ideal for enterprise skill allowlists.

violation_logging.description: Violation logging configuration (NEW in v1.1.0). Logs blocked operations to JSONL file for audit and review.

max_entries.description: Maximum number of log entries to retain

retention_days.description: Number of days to retain log entries

log_types.description: Types of violations to log. Empty array logs all types.

permission_rule.description: Permission rule defining allow/deny patterns for a specific tool matcher

mode.description: Permission mode: 'allow' creates an allow-list, 'deny' creates a deny-list

patterns.description: List of patterns to allow/deny. Supports simple strings or time-based objects with valid_until.

action.description: Action on violation (NEW in v1.7.0): 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot add or modify rules for this matcher. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

time_based_pattern.description: Pattern with optional time-based expiration (NEW in v1.3.0)

valid_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T12:00:00Z'). Pattern expires after this time.

time_based_feature.description: Feature toggle with optional time-based temporary disabling (NEW in v1.4.0)

disabled_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T18:00:00Z'). Feature override expires after this time.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the temporary change

directory_entry.description: Directory to scan for auto-discovery of tool permissions (NOT filesystem path blocking). Scans directory or GitHub repo for permission files and generates tool permission rules. Purpose: Auto-populate permissions.rules, not block directories. In object format (allow/deny arrays), mode is determined by which array the entry is in. In array format, mode defaults to 'allow' if not specified.

matcher.description: Tool type to apply discovered patterns to (e.g., 'Skill', 'mcp__*'). Defaults to 'Skill' if not specified.

mode.description: Permission mode for discovered patterns. Only used in array format; ignored in object format (allow/deny arrays). Defaults to 'allow'.

url.description: Local directory path or GitHub URL to scan

token_env.description: Environment variable containing GitHub token (for private repos)

remote_url_entry.description: Remote configuration URL with optional settings

url.description: Remote configuration URL (https://) or local file path

enabled.description: Whether this remote config is enabled

token_env.description: Environment variable containing auth token for remote URL
Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)
